articles.title: 'I wish this was AI': Parents outraged over abuse of toddlers at Bengaluru daycare
articles.description: Shocking allegations of toddler abuse at a Bengaluru daycare, operating on Capgemini's campus, have ignited outrage among working parents. Demands for livestreamed activities and constant surveillance are surging. The incident highlights a critical lack of oversight, leaving vulnerable children at risk and shattering parental trust in essential childcare support systems. Companies are now urged to implement stringent safety measures and robust governance for workplace daycares.

articles.title: 'We did something nobody knew': Trump details secret US operations near Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Trump revealed US forces have repeatedly destroyed Iran's radar systems, forcing Tehran to rebuild them multiple times. He detailed stealth operations along the coast and strikes in response to attacks on shipping.  Meanwhile, indirect talks in Doha concluded without a major breakthrough, focusing on maritime traffic and unfreezing funds, with nuclear program discussions expected later.

articles.title: Born 50 yrs after Kasparov! How Pratitee became India's lone medallist at World Youth Chess
articles.description: Born exactly 50 years after Garry Kasparov, 13-year-old chess prodigy and maths Olympiad star Pratitee Bordoloi scripted history by winning India's only medal at the 2026 FIDE World Youth Chess Championship. The story explores her extraordinary rise, analytical brilliance, disciplined upbringing, and the unique blend of academic excellence, humility, and competitive ambition shaping her journey.

A passionate follower of sports since childhood, he has covered everything from mainstream to niche disciplines. His career began in 2018 with SportsKeeda, followed by roles at Fansided and Get Football, where he primarily covered European football. articles.title: Tibetan protester sets himself on fire outside UN headquarters in New York City
articles.description: A man, identified as Lobga Rangzen, set himself ablaze outside the UN headquarters in Manhattan, carrying a Tibetan flag in a suspected protest. Witnesses reported seeing him engulfed in flames before being quickly attended to by emergency services. He was pronounced dead at a hospital. Leaflets found at the scene bore slogans like "China Out of Tibet," reflecting a long-standing independence movement.

articles.title: 68 women MPs in Japan had 2 toilets at parliament. After petition they have 4
articles.description: Japan's parliament is set to add two more women's toilet cubicles following a petition by 58 female MPs highlighting the shortage. This move addresses the under-representation of women in politics, where they hold only 68 out of 465 lower house seats. The expansion aims to alleviate long queues faced by female lawmakers and staff, reflecting a broader push for gender equality in public life.

articles.title: "Charlie never got his wish of finding romantic love": Candace Owens reignites Erika Kirk controversy with emotional Charlie Kirk claims
articles.description: Candace Owens is facing renewed criticism after a viral interview clip resurfaced in which she reflected on Charlie Kirk's personal life and claimed he never found the love he had long wanted. Her remarks also referenced his widow, Erika Kirk, prompting backlash on social media. Critics accused Owens of making unverified personal claims during a sensitive time, while supporters defended her comments as an expression of grief. Neither Erika Kirk nor Charlie Kirk's family has publicly responded.

articles.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei to skip father Ali Khamenei's funeral over security fears: Report
articles.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, will skip his father's funeral due to Israeli threats, according to a representative. This follows a stark warning from Iran's Foreign Minister to the US, citing Israeli remarks about Mojtaba Khamenei being "marked for death." Diplomatic talks in Doha reported positive progress on a Middle East MoU, with further discussions planned post-funeral. Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike on February 28.

articles.title: Gold eyes first weekly gain in five weeks, Silver advances on weaker dollar
Fuel-hit Russia bought gasoline produced by India’s Nayara Energy via traders: Report
articles.description: Russia is reportedly sourcing gasoline from India via international traders to counter fuel shortages stemming from Ukrainian attacks on its infrastructure. Nayara Energy, with a significant stake held by Russian oil giant Rosneft, is identified as a key supplier. While Indian officials state no direct sales, they acknowledge indirect sourcing through trading firms. This development follows reports of substantial gasoline shipments from India to Russia.

articles.title: Carlsberg files IPO papers confidentially
articles.description: Danish brewer Carlsberg has confidentially submitted draft IPO papers for its Indian operations to Sebi. While the timing and progress depend on regulatory approvals and market conditions, the offering is understood to be a secondary share sale. This move signals a potential boost to the IPO market, which is seeing increased activity with other major companies also preparing for listings.

articles.title: ICAI CA Foundation May 2026 result today at 11 am; check scorecard, qualifying marks and steps to download
articles.description: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) will announce the CA Foundation May 2026 result today at 11 am. Candidates can access their scorecards through the official websites using their registration number and roll number. The result will include subject-wise marks, overall status and other key details. Candidates securing at least 40 per cent in each paper and 50 per cent aggregate will qualify for the next stage, the CA Intermediate course.

articles.title: RPSC RAS 2026 registration closes today for 607 posts; check direct link, eligibility, fees and exam schedule here
articles.description: The Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC) is closing the online application process today, July 3, 2026, for the Rajasthan State and Subordinate Services Combined Competitive Examination 2026. The recruitment will fill 607 vacancies, including 192 State Services and 415 Subordinate Services posts. Candidates with a graduation degree can apply if they meet the prescribed age criteria. The preliminary examination is scheduled for December 6, 2026, while the mains examination will be conducted on May 1 and 2, 2027.

articles.title: Viksit Bharat Young Leaders Dialogue focuses on doctors' well-being, fitness and youth participation on National Doctors' Day
articles.description: The United Doctors Front, in collaboration with the Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports, organised the Viksit Bharat Young Leaders Dialogue 2026 at Government Medical College, Amritsar, on National Doctors' Day. The roundtable focused on doctors' working conditions, mental health, fitness and work-life balance, while encouraging youth participation in nation-building. The event also saw the launch of campaigns on drug de-addiction and organ donation, alongside discussions involving doctors, medical students and healthcare professionals.

The event also saw the launch of campaigns on drug de-addiction and organ donation, alongside discussions involving doctors, medical students and healthcare professionals.

articles.title: AIIMS CRE-5 Recruitment 2026: Last date today to apply for 1,484 Group B and C posts; apply here
articles.description: AIIMS CRE-5 Recruitment 2026: The online application process for AIIMS CRE-5 Recruitment 2026 closes today at 5:00 PM for 1,484 Group B and Group C posts across participating AIIMS and other Central Government institutes. The recruitment notification was issued on June 13, 2026, by AIIMS New Delhi. The Computer Based Test is scheduled for July 25 to July 27, 2026. The selection process includes CBT, skill test where applicable, merit list preparation, choice filling and document verification.

articles.title: Alpha Release Live Updates: Alia's film rakes in 3.06 cr in advance booking
articles.full_text: Dibyendu Bhattacharya, in an interview with WION, shared, "It was fantastic. Most of my scenes are with Bobby Deol, and he's an incredible human being, a complete gentleman. He's always smiling, warm, and very accepting. He was an absolute delight to work with. He's a darling. We spent a lot of time together during the shoot, whether it was filming, eating meals, or simply talking about different things of life." He added, "During the shoot, Bobby's father, veteran actor Dharmendra, passed away, which was an incredibly sad moment for him and for the industry. Through all those conversations, Bobby Deol and I developed a great rappo. I truly got to know him during the shoot. Before that, I didn't really know him, but now I've gotten to know him closely, and that's something I really value." The makers of the film dropped a video on social media in which Alia Bhatt can be heard saying, "Our film Alpha is releasing in theatres on 3rd July. We hope you are excited to watch it, but we have a small request. Please watch Alpha only in theatres. Do not watch or share pirated links, and please avoid giving out any spoilers of the film. Thank you, and see you in theatres." According to an Asianet News report, Alia Bhatt has reportedly charged Rs 25 crore for Alpha, the first female-led film in the YRF Spy Universe. The report claims her fee is nearly eight times higher than that of co-star Sharvari. Sharvari, who plays a key role alongside Alia in the spy thriller, reportedly earned Rs 3 crore for the film, reflecting a significant difference in the two actors' pay packages. AR Rahman took to his social media and wrote, "Best wishes to @aliaa08 and the entire team of #Alpha. @AnilKapoor @thedeol #sharvari @yrf @shivrawail #adityachopra #akshaywidhani" Alia Bhatt plays Sita, a fearless and lethal secret agent known as the Alpha Killer. Sharvari stars as a fellow spy who teams up with Sita on a high-stakes mission. Bobby Deol portrays the menacing Fateh Singh Lakhawat, Sita's mentor who trains her to become a deadly operative. Hrithik Roshan reprises his iconic YRF Spy Universe role as Major Kabir Dhaliwal in a special appearance. According to the NDTV report, before securing its UA certification from the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC), the makers of Alpha carried out several changes as directed by the board. The visuals at 25 minutes and 49 seconds were replaced with alternate shots, while a multiple stabbing sequence at 1 hour, 36 minutes and 41 seconds was toned down and substituted with modified visuals. Additionally, the CBFC directed the filmmakers to remove a foul word at 35 minutes and 53 seconds and alter another visual at 38 minutes and 12 seconds before clearing the film for theatrical release. Prabhas took to his Instagram Story to share the trailer and wrote, "Alpha trailer looks amazing and promising. Wishing the best to Alia, YRF, and the entire team for July 3." Alia reposted his story and added, "Thank you thank you thank you so much." In an interview with WION, Dibyendu Bhattacharya said, "It's fantastic, and it's a completely different world, the spy universe. As an actor, I don't like to limit or judge myself based on genres. Whether it's a niche film, a supernatural film, or a mainstream commercial movie, I want to do them all." The actor further shared, "And of course, I definitely want to do mainstream films because they bring you closer to a much wider audience. Mainstream cinema has an enormous reach. So yes, I was really excited about Alpha and the opportunity to work with Shiv Rawail." 'Alpha' is the first female-led film in Aditya Chopra's Spy Universe 'Alpha', starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari, is the seventh instalment in the Spy Universe backed by Aditya Chopra Alia Bhatt and Sharvari are the stars of the movie. While Alia's character name is Sita in the movie, Sharvari's name has not been revealed yet. The movie also stars Bobby Deol, Anil Kapoor, Dibyendu Bhattacharya, and Sangay Tsheltrim

articles.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donate $26M to charities
articles.description: In a remarkable show of generosity, pop icon Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce have pledged a stunning $26 million to support more than 20 charitable organizations across the country. Their contributions, aimed at aiding food banks, children's hospitals, and educational initiatives, emerge amid rumors suggesting an impending wedding in New York this weekend. Known for her extensive philanthropic efforts, Swift continues to uplift communities wherever she goes.

articles.title: 'Ramayana' Trailer: Makers plan July launch event?
articles.description: Excitement is building for Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana' epic, with fans eagerly anticipating a trailer launch this month. Reports suggest a grand event in Los Angeles on July 14 will unveil the first official trailer, nearly three months before the film's Diwali 2026 release. The ambitious project, starring Ranbir Kapoor, Yash, and Sai Pallavi, is also rumored to debut footage at San Diego Comic-Con.

articles.title: 'Sabki blessings chahenge': Aamir confirms July 5 wedding
articles.description: Aamir Khan, the beloved Bollywood superstar, excitedly announced his wedding to Gauri Spratt on July 5th, marking a significant milestone in their long-standing relationship. The couple will have an intimate ceremony at Khan's Mumbai residence, emphasizing the core family theme of their special day. With a rich history spanning 25 years of friendship, Khan is looking forward to this joyful new beginning and has humbly sought blessings from well-wishers.
